steps.title: Find the sentence's main verb
steps.content: Subject: Helical swimming. Main verb: bestows (.bestows similar advantages. to larger oceanic macroplanktons). The middle chunk (widely understood to confer stability.ciliates) is a parenthetical modifier set off by commas.

steps.title: Punctuate the parenthetical correctly
steps.content:
The other choices each insert a finite verb (has been, is) — that gives two main verbs with no conjunction.

We need only the participial phrase widely understood to confer stability. as a parenthetical, with a comma before it.

C uses just a comma — Helical swimming, widely understood to confer., bestows. — keeping bestows as the lone main verb. C is correct.
